# Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST Connector - Full Documentation

# Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST (Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST Connector)
enables you to integrate with the Salesforce Marketing Cloud marketing platform. This connector
exposes operations provided by the Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST API.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, refer to the Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST
Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

## Connection Types

Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST connections use the OAuth Client Credentials authentication type,
which enables a client to use its own credentials.

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, you are ready to create an app and configure the connector
using Anypoint Studio.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST Connector Reference](salesforce-marketing-cloud-rest-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST Connector 1.2 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

---

### Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_OauthClientCredentials">OAuth Client Credentials</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time in milliseconds that the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### OAuth Client Credentials

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Service URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL of the service on Salesforce Marketing Cloud to access from the connector. All other endpoints are derived from this base URL.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy configuration for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Socket connection timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, the user must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client secret as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://{domain}.auth.marketingcloudapis.com/v1/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Create Asset](#CreateAsset)
- [Create Email Send Definition](#CreateEmailSendDefinition)
- [Create Event Definition](#CreateEventDefinition)
- [Create Keyword](#CreateKeyword)
- [Create Sms Send Definition](#CreateSmsSendDefinition)
- [Delete Asset](#DeleteAsset)
- [Delete Email Send Definition](#DeleteEmailSendDefinition)
- [Delete Event Definition](#DeleteEventDefinition)
- [Delete Keyword By Id](#DeleteKeywordById)
- [Delete Keyword By Long Code](#DeleteKeywordByLongCode)
- [Delete Keyword By Short Code](#DeleteKeywordByShortCode)
- [Delete Sms Send Definition](#DeleteSmsSendDefinition)
- [Get Event Definition](#GetEventDefinition)
- [Send Email Message To Single Recipient](#SendEmailMessageToSingleRecipient)
- [Send Email Message To Multiple Recipients](#SendEmailMessageToMultipleRecipients)
- [Send Email Message Using Triggered Send Definition](#SendEmailMessageUsingTriggeredSendDefinition)
- [Send Sms Message To Single Recipient](#SendSmsMessageToSingleRecipient)
- [Send Sms Message To Multiple Recipients](#SendSmsMessageToMultipleRecipients)
- [Trigger Event](#TriggerEvent)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)
- [Update Event Definition](#UpdateEventDefinition)

## Create Asset

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:create-asset>`

Creates a draft of a new asset in either JSON or MIME format.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Asset Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>TEXT_ONLY_EMAIL</p></li><li><p>HTML_EMAIL</p></li><li><p>TEMPLATE_BASED_EMAIL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Asset type.</p></td><td><p>TEXT_ONLY_EMAIL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Asset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Asset to create.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Create Email Send Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:create-email-send-definition>`

Creates a long-living send definition object that references the email template, subscriber list,
sending options, journey, and metadata.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Email Send Definition</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Email send definition to create.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Create Event Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:create-event-definition>`

Creates an event definition (name and data schema for an event) and defines an event definition key.
The resource uses this key when firing an event to send it to the appropriate journey. Typically,
marketers create the event definition in the Journey Builder UI. Use this resource instead if youâre
using a custom application for Journey Builder functionality. To call this resource, assign your API
Integration the `Automation > Journeys/Interactions > Read` scope.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Definition</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Email send definition to create.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Create Keyword

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:create-keyword>`

Creates a keyword on an account.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keyword</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Keyword to create.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Create Sms Send Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:create-sms-send-definition>`

Creates an SMS send definition.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sms Send Definition</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>SMS send definition to create.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Delete Asset

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:delete-asset>`

Deletes an asset.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the asset to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Is Cdn Delete</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Permanently deletes the file and its URL in the CDN when the associated file is deleted in Content Builder. A value of <code>1</code> permanently deletes the file. An unspecified value or a value of <code>0</code> does not permanently delete the file.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Delete Email Send Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:delete-email-send-definition>`

Deletes a message definition. You cannot restore a deleted definition. The deleted definition is
archived, and the response provides a delete location of the definition key for reference. You can
reuse a deleted definition key because the information associated with the deleted definition key is
copied to a new unique identifier.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Definition Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Definition key to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Delete Event Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:delete-event-definition>`

Deletes an individual event definition by ID or key.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Definition Option</p></td><td><div><div><p>DefinitionOption</p></div></div></td><td><p>Definition key to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Delete Keyword By Id

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:delete-keyword-by-id>`

Deletes a keyword from an account.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keyword ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Keyword ID to delete.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:delete-keyword-by-long-code>`

Deletes a keyword from an account.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keyword</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Keyword to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Long code</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Long code of the keyword to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Delete Keyword By Short Code

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:delete-keyword-by-short-code>`

Deletes a keyword from an account.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keyword</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Keyword to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Short code</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Short code of the keyword to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Country code</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Country code of the keyword to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Delete Sms Send Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:delete-sms-send-definition>`

Deletes a message definition. You cannot restore a deleted definition. The deleted definition is
archived, and the response provides a delete location of the definition key for reference. You can
reuse a deleted definition key because the information associated with the deleted definition key is
copied to a new unique identifier.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Definition Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Definition key to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Get Event Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:get-event-definition>`

Retrieves an individual event definition by ID or key.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Definition Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the event definition expressed in the form of a GUID (UUID). Required if not using a key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Definition Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key of the event definition. Required if not using an ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Send Email Message To Multiple Recipients

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:send-email-message-to-multiple-recipients>`

Sends a message to multiple recipients using a send definition. You can provide a messageKey in the
request; otherwise, the messageKey is automatically generated in the response. The limit is 2000
recipients per request. If you send attributes with the recipients, we recommend no more than 500
recipients per request.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Email Request Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Email send definition to create.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Send Email Message To Single Recipient

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:send-email-message-to-single-recipient>`

Sends a message to a single recipient via a send definition using a **Message Key** path parameter.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier used to track the message status. Can be automatically created when you create a message or can be provided as part of the request. Can be up to 100 characters, and there are no restricted characters. Each recipient in a request must have a unique <strong>Message Key</strong>. If you use a duplicate <strong>Message Key</strong> in the same send request, the message is rejected.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Email Request Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content of the message to send.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Send Email Message Using Triggered Send Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:send-email-message-using-triggered-send-definition>`

Sends transactional email using Marketing Cloudâs triggered send functionality.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Triggered Send Email Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content of the message to send.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the event definition expressed in the form of a GUID (UUID). Required if not using a key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key of the event definition. Required if not using an ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
