## Send Sms Message To Multiple Recipients

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:send-sms-message-to-multiple-recipients>`

Sends a message to multiple recipients using a send definition. You can provide a messageKey in the
request; otherwise, the messageKey is automatically generated in the response. The limit is 2000
recipients per request. If you send attributes with the recipients, we recommend no more than 500
recipients per request.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content of the message to send.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Send Sms Message To Single Recipient

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:send-sms-message-to-single-recipient>`

Sends a message to a single recipient via a send definition using a **Message Key** path parameter.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier used to track the message status. Can be automatically created when you create a message or can be provided as part of the request. Can be up to 100 characters, and there are no restricted characters. Each recipient in a request must have a unique <strong>Message Key</strong>. If you use a duplicate <strong>Message Key</strong> in the same send request, the message is rejected.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content of the message to send.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Trigger Event

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:trigger-event>`

Fires the entry event that initiates the journey. If you use UTF-8 characters in a payload for the
Rest API, change the Content-Type header to: `application/json; charset=UTF-8`.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trigger Event Request Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content of the message to send.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour,</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Unauthorize

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that it is impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

## Update Event Definition

`<sfdc-marketing-cloud-rest:update-event-definition>`

Updates an event definition by ID or key. Once an event definition is created, only a few properties
can be updated. To call this resource, assign your API Integration the
`Automation | Interactions | Read` scope.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Definition Option</p></td><td><div><div><p>DefinitionOption</p></div></div></td><td><p>Definition key to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update Event Definition Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Event definition to update.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Overrides the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:BAD_REQUEST
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONFLICT
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:CONNECTIVITY
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:FORBIDDEN
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CONNECTION
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:NOT_FOUND
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:SERVER_ERROR
- SFDC-MARKETING-CLOUD-REST:TIMEOUT

## Types

### Proxy

Configures a proxy for outbound connections.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname or IP address of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Proxy Hosts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of hosts against which the proxy must not be used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ntlm Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Domain to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Definition Option

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the event definition expressed as a GUID (UUID). Required if not using a key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key of the event definition. Required if not using an ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount by which the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower specifies that the buffer canât expand.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
